steps.title: Identify what the blank must do grammatically
steps.content: The subject of the blank's clause is "tales of whose exploits, told and retold over time" — a noun phrase. The word after the blank is "myths" — the object. The blank needs a finite main verb to form a complete clause. Only a simple past-tense verb ("eventually became") can do that job.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'having eventually become': A perfect participle; creates a participial phrase, not an independent clause with a main verb. ✗
  • Choice B — 'eventually became': Simple past tense — gives the clause a main verb: "tales … eventually became myths." Matches the past tense of the main clause ("were based"). ✓
  • Choice C — 'to eventually become': An infinitive; cannot serve as the main verb of a clause. ✗
  • Choice D — 'eventually becoming': A present participle; creates a participial phrase with no finite verb. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
